_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot, Adam-supervised)
_meta.note: LOCAL refinement batch — Arlington Commonwealth's Attorney + 4 Chesapeake City Council. Roster verified June 2026. All TRUE/FALSE cite a source URL; null where no evidence. Prior speculative party-default cells reset to null.

profile.education: B.A. Philosophy & Comparative Literature, UC Berkeley; J.D., NYU School of Law
profile.background: Iranian-American (immigrated as a child); former Legal Director, Mid-Atlantic Innocence Project; former DC Public Defender Service (Special Litigation & Parole Divisions); first Iranian-American elected as a chief prosecutor in the U.S. Elected 2019 (defeated incumbent Theo Stamos in primary), reelected 2023 (defeated Josh Katcher 55.98–44.02 in Democratic primary; unopposed general); current term ends Jan 2028.

parisa-dehghani-tafti.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Commonwealth's Attorney for Arlington County + City of Falls Church through Jan 2028; subpoenaed by House Judiciary (3/20/2026) and named in formal sanctuary-policy investigation (5/4/2026). HARD evidence: (1) October 2020 — co-signed prosecutor joint statement pledging not to enforce abortion-criminalization laws (with Krasner, Boudin, Mosby, Rollins, Descano); (2) June 2025 Instagram: 'Reproductive freedom is FREEDOM. Proud to support REPRO Rising Virginia' — sanctity_of_life FALSE on both cells; (3) public_justice — declined to prosecute simple marijuana, formally ended cash bail, no-death-penalty pledge, established VA's first Conviction Review Unit + 'Heart of Safety' restorative-justice program, never certifies juveniles as adults, dismissed firearms charges in 2021 Reyes attempted-CVS-robbery case (Reyes later charged Dec 2024 with second-degree murder of Iman Gay at Pentagon City) — affirmative SOROS-funded ($621K+ Justice & Public Safety PAC in 2019) progressive prosecutor; (4) border — 2019 pledge of NO cooperation agreement with ICE, FAFO 'Fight Against Federal Overreach' coalition (Jan 2026) pledging to prosecute federal immigration officers, named cases Cabrera + Garcia Moran cited by House Judiciary as Arlington sanctuary failures. NO EVIDENCE on biblical_marriage individually (one restorative-justice Pride-flag-theft case in Dec 2024 is institutional posture only), family_child_sovereignty, christian_liberty, economic_stewardship, election_integrity, refuse_state_overreach (her resistance is to right-leaning AG/federal oversight, not to unconstitutional state mandates in the rubric sense — left null), self_defense (concealed-carry / red-flag positions). Religion: no church/denomination publicly identified.

0.note: Signed Oct 2020 joint progressive-prosecutor statement pledging not to enforce laws criminalizing abortion; June 2025 Instagram: 'Reproductive freedom is FREEDOM. Proud to support REPRO Rising Virginia.'

1.note: Affirmative pledge NOT to enforce abortion-criminalization laws — opposite of advocating abolition.

0.note: Supports Community Oversight Board + Critical Incident Response Team — police-oversight posture, not affirmative pro-LE advocacy. Public conflicts with AG Miyares over prosecutorial reforms; no documented affirmative pro-police statements.

1.note: Formally ended cash-bail in her office (Dec 2020); pledged no death penalty; Soros-funded (~$621K from Justice & Public Safety PAC in 2019) progressive prosecutor — the archetype the rubric calls out.

2.note: Established 'Heart of Safety' restorative-justice pilot + 'novel restorative justice program for young people'; dismissed firearms charges in 2021 Reyes attempted-robbery case (Reyes later charged with Dec 2024 murder of Iman Gay).

3.note: Public framing of crime via decarceration (cites $182/night jail cost, claims 30% lower jail population); declined-prosecution policies on marijuana possession; restorative rather than retributive default.

4.note: Pledged never to seek the death penalty; eliminated civil asset forfeiture; declines categories of prosecutions — actively limits the magistrate's punitive sword.

1.note: 2019 pledge to 'take immigration status into account when making charging decisions,' protect DACA status, and pursue 'resolutions that avoid deportation' — opposite of mandatory deportation.

2.note: 2019 pledge to 'never enter any cooperation agreement with ICE'; co-founded Jan 2026 FAFO coalition pledging to prosecute federal immigration officers; House Judiciary opened formal sanctuary-policy investigation 5/4/2026 naming her office.

profile.religion: Lifelong member, Kempsville Christian Church (Virginia Beach)
profile.education: B.A. Mathematics & Justice Studies, James Madison University; J.D., Regent University School of Law (Public Safety Scholarship); admitted VA Bar 2016
profile.background: Attorney (civil litigation, criminal/traffic, estate planning); former Chesapeake prosecutor under CA Nancy G. Parr; former 9-1-1 dispatcher; husband Brandon is a Chesapeake firefighter/paramedic. Endorsed-Republican Chesapeake council candidate (2022). At-Large; term expires Dec 31, 2026; up for reelection November 2026.

amanda-newins.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Chesapeake council member, term expires 12/31/2026; up for reelection Nov 2026. Endorsed-Republican (2022 Chesapeake GOP). Found NOT GUILTY by jury Oct 2023 of vulnerable-adult financial-exploitation charge. HARD evidence: (1) delivered council invocation 5/13/2025 (per official minutes) — christian_liberty q3 TRUE; (2) voted YES on unanimous 9-0 FY24 budget that CUT the real-estate tax rate 4 cents ($1.05 → $1.01); campaign priorities explicitly pledge 'lowest tax burden in Hampton Roads' and oppose tax hike for curbside recycling — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE; (3) campaign-stated 'fully fund our law enforcement and fire department so they can do the job they were trained to do — keep us safe!' + prosecutor career — public_justice q0 TRUE (affirmative advocacy bar met by explicit pro-LE pledge tied to professional history). EXCUSED (absent) from May 28, 2024 council meeting where Mayor West announced city's first-ever Pride proclamation (no council vote occurred) — biblical_marriage left null. NO EVIDENCE on sanctity_of_life, family_child_sovereignty (beyond generic 'fund schools' language), election_integrity (her NO vote on ward-system referendum was framed in localism/Dillon-Rule terms — does not map to q0/q1/q4), refuse_state_overreach (no COVID/ESG record), border_immigration (no individual statement), self_defense (worked at a gun range as personal/biographical fact — not a policy statement).

3.note: Delivered the council invocation at the May 13, 2025 Chesapeake City Council meeting (per official minutes); lifelong member of Kempsville Christian Church.

2.note: Voted YES on unanimous 9-0 FY24 budget that CUT real-estate tax rate by 4 cents ($1.05 → $1.01); campaign pledges 'maintain the lowest tax burden in all of Hampton Roads' and opposes new tax/fee for curbside recycling.

0.note: Explicit affirmative campaign pledge: 'fully fund our law enforcement and fire department so they can do the job they were trained to do — keep us safe!' Former Chesapeake prosecutor; husband is Chesapeake firefighter/paramedic.

profile.religion: Member, Lake Drummond Baptist Church (Chesapeake), with family
profile.education: Oscar Smith HS; Chesapeake Center for Science and Technology; Tidewater Community College
profile.background: Lifelong Chesapeake resident; master electrician (VA + NC); owner/president, D2W Electric, Inc.; husband and father of four. Campaigned door-to-door with his children for Youngkin/Sears/Miyares (2021). Currently appointed to Chesapeake Land Bank Authority. Sworn in Jan 1, 2023; term ends Dec 31, 2026; running for reelection Nov 2026 (committee filed as Independent with VPAP — Chesapeake council races are technically nonpartisan).

daniel-whitaker.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Chesapeake At-Large council member, sworn in 1/1/2023, term ends 12/31/2026, running for reelection 11/2026. HARD evidence: (1) personally delivered the council invocation Oct 22, 2024 AND Sept 9, 2025 (per official minutes) — christian_liberty q3 TRUE; (2) campaign-stated 'Public safety is his first priority' and pledge to keep first-responder pay among the highest in the region and equip them with necessary tools — public_justice q0 TRUE (explicit affirmative advocacy + concrete pay/equipment pledge); (3) cast the lone NO (8–1) vote on July 9, 2024 against placing a fee-bearing curbside-recycling referendum on the November 2024 ballot (voters ultimately rejected it); explicit campaign pledge for 'lower property and business taxes' with 'right budget and fiscal responsibility... without raising taxes' — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE; (4) recused June 17, 2025 from 7-0 council rejection of a 350,000 sq ft Emerald Lakes data-center rezoning (had provided electrical services to developer) — character signal, not directly scoreable. NO EVIDENCE on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Mayor West's 2024 Pride proclamation was mayoral, not a council vote), family_child_sovereignty (school issues handled by separate elected school board), election_integrity, refuse_state_overreach (generic 'uphold constitutional protections' is too vague to score; 2019 2A vote predates his tenure), border_immigration (Chesapeake's 2017 sanctuary-substitute vote predates his tenure), self_defense (June 2025 firearms-discharge nuisance ordinance is narrow public-safety, not gun-control proper; 2019 2A Constitutional City vote predates his tenure).

3.note: Personally delivered the council invocation on Oct 22, 2024 (Regular Meeting) and Sept 9, 2025 (Work Session) per official Chesapeake minutes; member of Lake Drummond Baptist Church.

2.note: Campaign pledge to 'fight for lower property and business taxes... without raising taxes'; cast the lone NO (8-1) vote against placing a fee-bearing curbside-recycling referendum on the November 2024 ballot.

0.note: Explicit campaign-stated 'Public safety is his first priority' and pledge to keep first-responders' compensation 'among the highest in the region' and 'equipping them with necessary tools' — affirmative pro-LE advocacy with concrete pay/equipment commitment.

profile.background: Born Newport, RI (Navy family); moved to Hampton Roads 1977 via Navy orders, settled in Great Bridge with retired-Navy husband Ron. Member, Central Chesapeake Republican Women's Club. Chaired DoD Joint Land Use Study on NAS Oceana/Fentress (2005); past president, Women's Division of Hampton Roads Chamber of Commerce Chesapeake; Executive Board, Virginia Coalition of High Growth Localities; Governor-appointed Opioid Abatement Authority (2025-). First elected May 1998; reelected Nov 5, 2024; appointed Vice Mayor 8-1 on Sept 24, 2025, replacing the late John de Triquet. Current term ends Dec 31, 2028.

debbie-ritter.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Vice Mayor of Chesapeake, appointed 8-1 on Sept 24, 2025 to replace late John de Triquet; first elected 1998 (27+ years); current term ends Dec 31, 2028. HARD evidence: (1) signature campaign claim — 'ONLY Southside city with NO real-estate tax rate increase in over 20 years' — corroborated by unanimous FY26 $1.7B budget adopted with NO rate hike (kept at $1.01/$100); publicly raised concerns about 'duplication of efforts' in Human Services grant spending — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE; (2) explicit campaign-stated 'no higher priority than a safe City'; advocated repeal of state laws constraining police authority to confiscate illegal guns/drugs; supported new LE/first-responder pay plan, new training academy with compressed phasing, and new fire/police stations — public_justice q0 TRUE (affirmative advocacy bar met); same record + anti-soft-on-crime stance via repeal of LE-restricting state laws — public_justice q1 TRUE; (3) advocacy for repealing state laws that 'tie the hands of law enforcement' = local-control assertion against state preemption — refuse_state_overreach q0 TRUE; serves on Executive Board of Virginia Coalition of High Growth Localities — refuse_state_overreach q3 TRUE (subsidiarity / local-control institutional posture); (4) sitting council member during unanimous Dec 10, 2019 council declaration of Chesapeake as Second Amendment 'Constitutional City' (vote was unanimous and Ritter was on council) — self_defense q1 TRUE. NO EVIDENCE on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Mayor West's 2024 Pride proclamation was mayoral, not a council vote — left null), family_child_sovereignty (school issues are handled by separate elected school board), christian_liberty (no personal invocation/policy quote located), election_integrity, refuse_state_overreach q1/q2/q4 (no specific COVID/ESG votes located), border_immigration (Ritter's individual vote on 2017 8-1 sanctuary-substitute and 2024 6-3 ward-referendum not confirmed from retrieved sources), self_defense q0 (no statement on constitutional carry). No church/religion publicly identified.

2.note: Signature campaign claim of NO real-estate tax rate increase in 20+ years; voted with unanimous FY26 $1.7B budget that kept the rate flat at $1.01/$100; publicly raised concerns about 'duplication of efforts' in Human Services grant spending during budget deliberations.

0.note: Campaign-stated 'There is no higher priority than a safe City'; supported new pay plan to recruit/retain LE and first responders; supported new training academy with compressed construction phasing; supported new fire/police stations — affirmative pro-LE advocacy with concrete pay + facility commitments.

1.note: Explicit campaign-stated advocacy 'for repeal of State Laws that tie the hands of law enforcement to confiscate illegal guns and drugs' — opposite of soft-on-crime / rogue-prosecutor posture.

0.note: Advocates repeal of state laws constraining local police authority to confiscate illegal guns/drugs — explicit assertion of local control against state preemption of local LE.

3.note: Serves on the Executive Board of the Virginia Coalition of High Growth Localities — institutional local-control / subsidiarity advocacy posture.

1.note: Sitting council member during the Dec 10, 2019 UNANIMOUS council vote declaring Chesapeake a Second Amendment 'Constitutional City' — explicit pro-2A posture opposing state-imposed gun restrictions.

profile.religion: Member (church greeter), Mt. Lebanon Missionary Baptist Church
profile.education: B.A. English (Norfolk State University, 1969, valedictorian); M.A. Mass Communications (NSU, 1977); M.S. Educational Administration (Old Dominion, 1993); CAGS + EdD Educational Administration (Virginia Tech, 1999–2000)
profile.background: Retired educator (Portsmouth Public Schools, 35 years — 23 yrs English/Journalism teacher Wilson HS, 12 yrs Asst Principal at Waters/Cradock Middle); 6 yrs Chesapeake School Board; 8 yrs Virginia Board of Education (5 as Vice President); CNU Board of Visitors; HRPDC Chair; HRTPO. First elected Chesapeake City Council May 2006; reelected 2010, 2014, 2018, 2022. 2012 Democratic nominee for U.S. House VA-04 (lost to Randy Forbes). Member, Chesapeake Democratic Committee. Current term ends Jan 1, 2027; running for reelection Nov 2026.

ella-ward.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Chesapeake council member, term ends Jan 1, 2027; running for reelection Nov 2026 (campaign launched at June 5 picnic attended by Rep. Bobby Scott). Openly affiliated Democrat (Chesapeake Democratic Committee; 2012 Democratic nominee VA-04). HARD evidence: voted YES on unanimous 9-0 FY24 budget that CUT the real-estate tax rate 4 cents ($1.05 → $1.01); supported VM de Triquet's 2022 motion to cut RE tax rate 4.5 cents (motion failed 4-5); voted NO on 2023 toll-rate increase on Chesapeake Expressway/Dominion Boulevard Veterans Bridge — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE (consistently pro-taxpayer at the rate-vote level). Publicly favors changing Chesapeake from at-large to ward/borough voting structure ('The way we elect council is a barrier for poor and working class people'). Present at May 28, 2024 council meeting where Mayor West announced Chesapeake's first-ever Pride proclamation (no council vote; no recorded objection). NO EVIDENCE individually scored on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (no Ward vote/statement; mayoral proclamation only), family_child_sovereignty (her 'fund schools' framing is generic public-education-establishment posture, not a parental-rights/CRT/choice statement), christian_liberty (faith identity established but no specific dated invocation), election_integrity (her early-voting expansion vote 5/28/2024 expands access, doesn't map to q0/q1/q4; ward-system support is structural-equity, not voter-ID/paper-ballot/Zuckerbucks), public_justice ('equitable public safety pay raises' is budget-level, doesn't clear the affirmative-advocacy bar), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration, self_defense (2019 2A Constitutional City vote was unanimous and Ward was on council, but her presence at that specific meeting was not confirmed in retrieved sources — left null pending minutes review).

2.note: Voted YES on unanimous 9-0 FY24 budget that CUT the real-estate tax rate 4 cents ($1.05 → $1.01); supported VM de Triquet's 2022 motion to cut RE tax rate 4.5 cents (failed 4-5); voted NO on 2023 toll-rate increase on Chesapeake Expressway/Dominion Boulevard Veterans Bridge.
